evals.prompt: I'm building a Next.js app with shadcn/ui (base-nova preset, lucide icons). Create a settings form component with fields for: full name, email address, and notification preferences (email, SMS, push notifications as toggle options). Add validation states for required fields.
evals.expected_output: A React component using FieldGroup, Field, ToggleGroup, data-invalid/aria-invalid validation, gap-* spacing, and semantic colors.
expectations: Uses FieldGroup and Field components for form layout instead of raw div with space-y
Uses Switch for independent on/off notification toggles (not looping Button with manual active state)
Uses data-invalid on Field and aria-invalid on the input control for validation states
Uses gap-* (e.g. gap-4, gap-6) instead of space-y-* or space-x-* for spacing
Uses semantic color tokens (e.g. bg-background, text-muted-foreground, text-destructive) instead of raw colors like bg-red-500
No manual dark: color overrides

evals.prompt: Create a dialog component for editing a user profile. It should have the user's avatar at the top, input fields for name and bio, and Save/Cancel buttons with appropriate icons. Using shadcn/ui with radix-nova preset and tabler icons.
evals.expected_output: A React component with DialogTitle, Avatar+AvatarFallback, data-icon on icon buttons, no icon sizing classes, tabler icon imports.
expectations: Includes DialogTitle for accessibility (visible or with sr-only class)
Avatar component includes AvatarFallback
Icons on buttons use the data-icon attribute (data-icon="inline-start" or data-icon="inline-end")
No sizing classes on icons inside components (no size-4, w-4, h-4, etc.)
Uses tabler icons (@tabler/icons-react) instead of lucide-react
Uses asChild for custom triggers (radix preset)

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard component that shows 4 stat cards in a grid. Each card has a title, large number, percentage change badge, and a loading skeleton state. Using shadcn/ui with base-nova preset and lucide icons.
evals.expected_output: A React component with full Card composition, Skeleton for loading, Badge for changes, semantic colors, gap-* spacing.
expectations: Uses full Card composition with CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent (not dumping everything into CardContent)
Uses Skeleton component for loading placeholders instead of custom animate-pulse divs
Uses Badge component for percentage change instead of custom styled spans
Uses semantic color tokens instead of raw color values like bg-green-500 or text-red-600
Uses gap-* instead of space-y-* or space-x-* for spacing
Uses size-* when width and height are equal instead of separate w-* h-*

evals.prompt: Build a pricing page for my SaaS analytics product using shadcn/ui (base-nova preset, lucide icons, Next.js App Router). Three tiers: Starter, Pro, Enterprise. The Pro plan should be highlighted. Include a monthly/annual toggle and a FAQ section below. Make it look professional and polished — not generic.
evals.expected_output: A pricing page with intentional color choices (not default purple gradient), distinctive typography, clear visual hierarchy highlighting the Pro plan, ToggleGroup for billing period, Accordion for FAQ, and semantic color usage throughout.
expectations: Uses a deliberate color palette with CSS variables (not raw Tailwind colors) — primary for the highlighted plan CTA, muted/secondary for others
Recommends or uses a specific font pairing beyond the system default (e.g. a Google Fonts import for headings)
Pro plan card has clear visual distinction (size, border, background, or Badge) creating hierarchy over other tiers
Uses ToggleGroup for monthly/annual billing toggle instead of custom button state management
Uses Accordion for FAQ section instead of custom show/hide divs
Varies spacing intentionally — tighter within cards, generous between pricing grid and FAQ section
Does not use a purple-on-white gradient or other generic AI aesthetic pattern

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard layout for an internal DevOps monitoring tool. It needs: a collapsible sidebar with navigation, a header with search and user avatar, a main area with 3 metric cards at the top, a large chart showing request latency over time, and a table of recent incidents below. Using shadcn/ui with base-nova preset, lucide icons, Next.js. Dark mode should be the default.
evals.expected_output: A dashboard with dark-mode-first design, technical/utilitarian aesthetic, Sidebar component, Chart with themed --chart-* colors, data Table, metric Cards with proper hierarchy, and responsive considerations.
expectations: Uses Sidebar component for navigation (not a custom div-based sidebar)
Uses Chart component (Recharts wrapper) for the latency chart with --chart-* CSS variable theming
Metric cards use full Card composition (CardHeader/CardTitle/CardContent) with Badge for status
Dark mode is configured as default (not just supported) — color choices work in dark theme
Information density is appropriate for a monitoring tool — compact spacing within data areas, not overly spacious
Table uses the Table component with proper structure, not a custom grid
Avatar includes AvatarFallback in the header
Mentions or implements responsive behavior (e.g. Sidebar collapsing, Table adapting on mobile)

evals.prompt: Build a portfolio landing page for a freelance photographer named Sarah Chen. It should have a hero section with her name and tagline, a filterable gallery grid of her work (categories: portrait, landscape, street), a brief about section, and a contact form. Use shadcn/ui with base-nova preset and lucide icons on Next.js. I want it to feel artistic and distinctive, not like a template.
evals.expected_output: A portfolio page with bold aesthetic direction — distinctive font pairing (e.g. serif headings), intentional color palette, generous whitespace, entrance animations on gallery items, and artistic layout choices that don't feel cookie-cutter.
expectations: Uses a distinctive font pairing appropriate for a creative portfolio (e.g. serif or display heading font, not just system sans-serif)
Color palette suits a creative/artistic context — not a corporate blue or generic purple
Gallery uses ToggleGroup for category filtering instead of custom button state
Contact form uses FieldGroup + Field composition with proper validation
Hero section has visual impact — generous sizing, strong typography hierarchy, not just centered small text
Includes entrance animation or transition effect on gallery items (staggered reveal, fade-in, etc.) with motion-safe prefix
Layout has at least one non-standard element (asymmetric grid, full-bleed section, overlapping elements) to avoid template feel
About section uses controlled line length (max-w-prose or similar) for readability
